mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, prompting forward-looking equity investors to price in imminent corporate earnings growth over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases yield negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or regime-specific IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, immediately boosting equity valuations as earnings expectations are revised upward over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following strong GDP prints are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant or rising federal funds rates indicate tightening real monetary conditions, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPI and stable high rates fails to predict negative returns (Rank IC > 0.02) or positive returns occur in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls during a post-shock recovery phase signals strengthening economic momentum and rising wage income, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth during recovery phases yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of instances or loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and reduced business investment, immediately pressuring equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to produce negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases or Rank IC remains above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
